channel.title: TOOL_PTK on ZAP
channel.description: Recent content in TOOL_PTK on ZAP

item.title: .NET stack trace / YSOD
item.description:
Detects common framework stack traces, error pages, and path disclosures in observed responses.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: access_token/id_token in URL
item.description:
Detects access tokens, JWTs, and API keys present in URLs or query strings observed in traffic.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Admin/management path observed
item.description:
Flags high-value endpoint patterns observed in traffic (admin panels, debug endpoints, consoles, and backup/config file paths).

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Android assetlinks.json observed
item.description:
Flags security-relevant well-known resources and metadata files when they appear in observed traffic.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: API docs endpoint observed
item.description:
Detects exposure of API documentation, specs, and interactive consoles observed in traffic.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: api_key/key in URL
item.description:
Detects access tokens, JWTs, and API keys present in URLs or query strings observed in traffic.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Apple app-site-association observed
item.description:
Flags security-relevant well-known resources and metadata files when they appear in observed traffic.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Avoid eval with string literals
item.description:
Detects dynamic execution of attacker-controlled strings in JavaScript sinks such as eval(), Function(), string-based timers, execScript, or script.text assignments. Exploiting these flows lets attackers execute arbitrary JS without relying on HTML injection.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: Avoid execScript dynamic execution
item.description:
Detects dynamic execution of attacker-controlled strings in JavaScript sinks such as eval(), Function(), string-based timers, execScript, or script.text assignments. Exploiting these flows lets attackers execute arbitrary JS without relying on HTML injection.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: Avoid Function constructor with strings
item.description:
Detects dynamic execution of attacker-controlled strings in JavaScript sinks such as eval(), Function(), string-based timers, execScript, or script.text assignments. Exploiting these flows lets attackers execute arbitrary JS without relying on HTML injection.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: Avoid string-based timers
item.description:
Detects dynamic execution of attacker-controlled strings in JavaScript sinks such as eval(), Function(), string-based timers, execScript, or script.text assignments. Exploiting these flows lets attackers execute arbitrary JS without relying on HTML injection.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: AWS Access Key ID pattern
item.description:
Flags secret-like tokens and exposed configuration values in observed HTML/JS/JSON responses. These are recon leads; validate sensitivity before reporting.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Cache-Control public/max-age with Set-Cookie
item.description:
Flags potentially risky cacheability for responses that appear user-specific and missing cache partitioning indicators.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Clear-Site-Data present but missing executionContexts
item.description:
The OWASP Secure Headers Project describes HTTP response headers that your application can use to increase the security of your application. Once set, these HTTP response headers can restrict modern browsers from running into easily preventable vulnerabilities.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Clear-Site-Data uses wildcard *
item.description:
The OWASP Secure Headers Project describes HTTP response headers that your application can use to increase the security of your application. Once set, these HTTP response headers can restrict modern browsers from running into easily preventable vulnerabilities.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Cloud metadata IP referenced
item.description:
Detects internal hostnames/IPs and environment hints (staging/dev/local) disclosed in observed responses.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: COEP present but value is not 'require-corp' or 'credentialless'
item.description:
The OWASP Secure Headers Project describes HTTP response headers that your application can use to increase the security of your application. Once set, these HTTP response headers can restrict modern browsers from running into easily preventable vulnerabilities.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: COOP present but value is not 'same-origin'
item.description:
The OWASP Secure Headers Project describes HTTP response headers that your application can use to increase the security of your application. Once set, these HTTP response headers can restrict modern browsers from running into easily preventable vulnerabilities.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: COOP set without COEP/CORP (incomplete cross-origin isolation)
item.description:
The OWASP Secure Headers Project describes HTTP response headers that your application can use to increase the security of your application. Once set, these HTTP response headers can restrict modern browsers from running into easily preventable vulnerabilities.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: CORS allows any origin with credentials
item.description:
The OWASP Secure Headers Project describes HTTP response headers that your application can use to increase the security of your application. Once set, these HTTP response headers can restrict modern browsers from running into easily preventable vulnerabilities.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: CORS allows broad headers
item.description:
Adds passive CORS posture checks: missing Vary: Origin for dynamic ACAO, and permissive allowed headers/methods.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: CORS allows broad methods
item.description:
Adds passive CORS posture checks: missing Vary: Origin for dynamic ACAO, and permissive allowed headers/methods.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Credit Card Number
item.description: Sensitive data is anything that should not be accessible to admin access, known as sensitive data. Sensitive data may include personally identifiable information (PII), such as Social Security numbers, financial information, or login credentials. Sensitive Data Exposure occurs when an organization unknowingly exposes sensitive data or when a security incident leads to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, or admin disclosure of, or access to sensitive data.

item.title: CSP 'frame-ancestors' missing or overly broad
item.description:
The OWASP Secure Headers Project describes HTTP response headers that your application can use to increase the security of your application. Once set, these HTTP response headers can restrict modern browsers from running into easily preventable vulnerabilities.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: CSP allows inline/eval or wildcards in script/style
item.description:
The OWASP Secure Headers Project describes HTTP response headers that your application can use to increase the security of your application. Once set, these HTTP response headers can restrict modern browsers from running into easily preventable vulnerabilities.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: CSP Report-Only present without enforcing CSP
item.description:
The OWASP Secure Headers Project describes HTTP response headers that your application can use to increase the security of your application. Once set, these HTTP response headers can restrict modern browsers from running into easily preventable vulnerabilities.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: data: URL assigned to script.src
item.description:
Tainted data: URL assigned to script.src and treated as executable content.

Generated by OWASP PTK IAST Module

item.title: Debug/diagnostic path observed
item.description:
Flags high-value endpoint patterns observed in traffic (admin panels, debug endpoints, consoles, and backup/config file paths).

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Deprecated Feature-Policy or unknown/overly-permissive Permissions-Policy
item.description:
The OWASP Secure Headers Project describes HTTP response headers that your application can use to increase the security of your application. Once set, these HTTP response headers can restrict modern browsers from running into easily preventable vulnerabilities.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Disallow direct document.cookie assignment (incl. bracket access)
item.description:
Detects cases where attacker-controlled DOM data is written into cookies (document.cookie or common wrapper functions). Can indicate session fixation, logic control, or preparation for exploit-chains.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: Disallow direct navigation primitives
item.description:
Detects client-side code that can redirect users to attacker-controlled URLs (open redirects). Includes assignment/calls that control window/location/navigation, attr-based redirects, form actions and jQuery variants.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: Disallow document.write()/writeln()
item.description:
Detects cases where untrusted data from the DOM (URL, element values, storage, messages, etc.) flows into HTML/JS execution sinks (e.g., innerHTML, outerHTML, document.write, string-based setTimeout, insertAdjacentHTML) without proper sanitization or encoding \u2014 enabling DOM-based cross-site scripting.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: Disallow innerHTML/outerHTML assignments
item.description:
Detects cases where untrusted data from the DOM (URL, element values, storage, messages, etc.) flows into HTML/JS execution sinks (e.g., innerHTML, outerHTML, document.write, string-based setTimeout, insertAdjacentHTML) without proper sanitization or encoding \u2014 enabling DOM-based cross-site scripting.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: Disallow insertAdjacentHTML()
item.description:
Detects cases where untrusted data from the DOM (URL, element values, storage, messages, etc.) flows into HTML/JS execution sinks (e.g., innerHTML, outerHTML, document.write, string-based setTimeout, insertAdjacentHTML) without proper sanitization or encoding \u2014 enabling DOM-based cross-site scripting.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: DOM XSS via document.write
item.description:
Tainted data passed to document.write.

Generated by OWASP PTK IAST Module

item.title: DOM XSS via DOM mutations
item.description:
Tainted data inserted into the DOM via DOM mutation APIs.

Generated by OWASP PTK IAST Module

item.title: DOM XSS via Element.innerHTML
item.description:
Tainted data assigned to innerHTML (possible DOM XSS).

Generated by OWASP PTK IAST Module

item.title: DOM XSS via Element.outerHTML
item.description:
Tainted data assigned to outerHTML (possible DOM XSS).

Generated by OWASP PTK IAST Module

item.title: DOM XSS via inline event handler
item.description:
Tainted data flowed into an inline event handler.

Generated by OWASP PTK IAST Module

item.title: DOM XSS via innerHTML (Angular)
item.description:
Detects cases where untrusted data from the DOM (URL, element values, storage, messages, etc.) flows into HTML/JS execution sinks (e.g., innerHTML, outerHTML, document.write, string-based setTimeout, insertAdjacentHTML) without proper sanitization or encoding \u2014 enabling DOM-based cross-site scripting.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: DOM XSS via insertAdjacentHTML
item.description:
Tainted HTML passed into insertAdjacentHTML.

Generated by OWASP PTK IAST Module

item.title: DOM-based Cookie Manipulation (taint flow)
item.description:
Detects cases where attacker-controlled DOM data is written into cookies (document.cookie or common wrapper functions). Can indicate session fixation, logic control, or preparation for exploit-chains.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: DOM-based JavaScript Injection (taint flow)
item.description:
Detects dynamic execution of attacker-controlled strings in JavaScript sinks such as eval(), Function(), string-based timers, execScript, or script.text assignments. Exploiting these flows lets attackers execute arbitrary JS without relying on HTML injection.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: DOM-based Open Redirection (taint flow)
item.description:
Detects client-side code that can redirect users to attacker-controlled URLs (open redirects). Includes assignment/calls that control window/location/navigation, attr-based redirects, form actions and jQuery variants.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: DOM-based XSS (taint flow)
item.description:
Detects cases where untrusted data from the DOM (URL, element values, storage, messages, etc.) flows into HTML/JS execution sinks (e.g., innerHTML, outerHTML, document.write, string-based setTimeout, insertAdjacentHTML) without proper sanitization or encoding \u2014 enabling DOM-based cross-site scripting.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: Dynamic ACAO without Vary: Origin
item.description:
Adds passive CORS posture checks: missing Vary: Origin for dynamic ACAO, and permissive allowed headers/methods.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Dynamic code execution via eval
item.description:
Tainted string executed via eval().

Generated by OWASP PTK IAST Module

item.title: Dynamic code execution via Function constructor
item.description:
Tainted string executed via Function constructor.

Generated by OWASP PTK IAST Module

item.title: Dynamic code execution via Function.apply
item.description:
Tainted string executed via Function.apply.

Generated by OWASP PTK IAST Module

item.title: Environment hints (dev/staging/test) in response
item.description:
Detects internal hostnames/IPs and environment hints (staging/dev/local) disclosed in observed responses.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Environment/config file observed
item.description:
Flags high-value endpoint patterns observed in traffic (admin panels, debug endpoints, consoles, and backup/config file paths).

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Expect-CT is deprecated
item.description:
The OWASP Secure Headers Project describes HTTP response headers that your application can use to increase the security of your application. Once set, these HTTP response headers can restrict modern browsers from running into easily preventable vulnerabilities.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Exposure of Git repository
item.description: Version control repositories such as CVS or git store version-specific metadata and other details within subdirectories. If these subdirectories are stored on a web server or added to an archive, then these could be used by an attacker. This information may include usernames, filenames, path root, IP addresses, and detailed ‘diff’ data about how files have been changed - which could reveal source code snippets that were never intended to be made public..

item.title: Exposure of Mercurial repository
item.description: Version control repositories such as CVS or git store version-specific metadata and other details within subdirectories. If these subdirectories are stored on a web server or added to an archive, then these could be used by an attacker. This information may include usernames, filenames, path root, IP addresses, and detailed ‘diff’ data about how files have been changed - which could reveal source code snippets that were never intended to be made public..

item.title: Exposure of SVN repository
item.description: Version control repositories such as CVS or git store version-specific metadata and other details within subdirectories. If these subdirectories are stored on a web server or added to an archive, then these could be used by an attacker. This information may include usernames, filenames, path root, IP addresses, and detailed ‘diff’ data about how files have been changed - which could reveal source code snippets that were never intended to be made public..

item.title: File/path candidate parameter
item.description:
Flags request parameters and JSON keys commonly associated with high-impact findings (open redirect, SSRF, IDOR, file/path traversal).

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Firebase config exposed
item.description:
Flags secret-like tokens and exposed configuration values in observed HTML/JS/JSON responses. These are recon leads; validate sensitivity before reporting.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Form action manipulated by tainted route or body input
item.description:
Tainted route, body, or messaging value changed form action.

item.title: HSTS max-age too low or missing includeSubDomains
item.description:
The OWASP Secure Headers Project describes HTTP response headers that your application can use to increase the security of your application. Once set, these HTTP response headers can restrict modern browsers from running into easily preventable vulnerabilities.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: HTML references .map files
item.description:
Detects source map references and common debug artifacts in observed HTML/JS responses. These are high-value recon leads for code disclosure and hidden endpoints.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: IDOR candidate parameter
item.description:
Flags request parameters and JSON keys commonly associated with high-impact findings (open redirect, SSRF, IDOR, file/path traversal).

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Inline event handler built from dynamic data

Detects cases where untrusted data from the DOM (URL, element values, storage, messages, etc.) flows into HTML/JS execution sinks (e.g., innerHTML, outerHTML, document.write, string-based setTimeout, insertAdjacentHTML) without proper sanitization or encoding \u2014 enabling DOM-based cross-site scripting.

Detects common framework stack traces, error pages, and path disclosures in observed responses.

Detects common framework stack traces, error pages, and path disclosures in observed responses.

item.title: JWT None Algorithm (Authorization header)
item.description:
This attack occurs when an attacker alters the token and changes the hashing algorithm to indicate, through the none keyword, that the integrity of the token has already been verified

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: JWT None Algorithm (Cookie)
item.description:
This attack occurs when an attacker alters the token and changes the hashing algorithm to indicate, through the none keyword, that the integrity of the token has already been verified

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.description:

item.title: Missing Content-Security-Policy header
item.description:
The OWASP Secure Headers Project describes HTTP response headers that your application can use to increase the security of your application. Once set, these HTTP response headers can restrict modern browsers from running into easily preventable vulnerabilities.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Missing or invalid X-Content-Type-Options
item.description:
The OWASP Secure Headers Project describes HTTP response headers that your application can use to increase the security of your application. Once set, these HTTP response headers can restrict modern browsers from running into easily preventable vulnerabilities.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Missing or weak Referrer-Policy
item.description:
The OWASP Secure Headers Project describes HTTP response headers that your application can use to increase the security of your application. Once set, these HTTP response headers can restrict modern browsers from running into easily preventable vulnerabilities.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Missing Strict-Transport-Security header (on HTTPS)
item.description:
The OWASP Secure Headers Project describes HTTP response headers that your application can use to increase the security of your application. Once set, these HTTP response headers can restrict modern browsers from running into easily preventable vulnerabilities.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.description:

Flags request parameters and JSON keys commonly associated with high-impact findings (open redirect, SSRF, IDOR, file/path traversal).

item.title: Open redirect via Navigation API
item.description:
Tainted destination URL used in navigation.navigate.

Generated by OWASP PTK IAST Module

item.title: Open redirect via window.open
item.description:
Tainted URL used in window.open (possible open redirect).

Generated by OWASP PTK IAST Module

item.title: OpenAPI spec detected
item.description:
Detects exposure of API documentation, specs, and interactive consoles observed in traffic.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: OS Command Injection - Unix cat /etc/passwd (pipe)
item.description:
OS command injection (also known as shell injection) is a web security vulnerability that allows an attacker to execute arbitrary operating system (OS) commands on the server that is running an application, and typically fully compromise the application and all its data.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: PHP fatal error / warning
item.description:
Detects common framework stack traces, error pages, and path disclosures in observed responses.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: phpinfo endpoint observed
item.description:
Flags high-value endpoint patterns observed in traffic (admin panels, debug endpoints, consoles, and backup/config file paths).

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Potential .git exposure path observed
item.description:
Flags high-value endpoint patterns observed in traffic (admin panels, debug endpoints, consoles, and backup/config file paths).

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Potential backup file observed
item.description:
Flags high-value endpoint patterns observed in traffic (admin panels, debug endpoints, consoles, and backup/config file paths).

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Potentially authenticated content lacks no-store
item.description:
The OWASP Secure Headers Project describes HTTP response headers that your application can use to increase the security of your application. Once set, these HTTP response headers can restrict modern browsers from running into easily preventable vulnerabilities.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Private key material exposed
item.description:
Flags secret-like tokens and exposed configuration values in observed HTML/JS/JSON responses. These are recon leads; validate sensitivity before reporting.

item.title: Prototype pollution influenced fetch() init
item.description:
A prior tainted prototype write influenced inherited fetch() init fields.

item.description:
The OWASP Secure Headers Project describes HTTP response headers that your application can use to increase the security of your application. Once set, these HTTP response headers can restrict modern browsers from running into easily preventable vulnerabilities.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

Detects common framework stack traces, error pages, and path disclosures in observed responses.

item.title: Review DOMParser.parseFromString with dynamic HTML/XML
item.description:
Detects cases where untrusted data from the DOM (URL, element values, storage, messages, etc.) flows into HTML/JS execution sinks (e.g., innerHTML, outerHTML, document.write, string-based setTimeout, insertAdjacentHTML) without proper sanitization or encoding \u2014 enabling DOM-based cross-site scripting.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.description:
Detects cases where untrusted data from the DOM (URL, element values, storage, messages, etc.) flows into HTML/JS execution sinks (e.g., innerHTML, outerHTML, document.write, string-based setTimeout, insertAdjacentHTML) without proper sanitization or encoding \u2014 enabling DOM-based cross-site scripting.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.description:

Generated by OWASP PTK IAST Module

item.title: Same-origin URL mutations
item.description:
Detects client-side code that can redirect users to attacker-controlled URLs (open redirects). Includes assignment/calls that control window/location/navigation, attr-based redirects, form actions and jQuery variants.

item.title: security.txt observed
item.description:
Flags security-relevant well-known resources and metadata files when they appear in observed traffic.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Sensitive cookies missing security flags

The OWASP Secure Headers Project describes HTTP response headers that your application can use to increase the security of your application. Once set, these HTTP response headers can restrict modern browsers from running into easily preventable vulnerabilities.

item.description:
Flags secret-like tokens and exposed configuration values in observed HTML/JS/JSON responses. These are recon leads; validate sensitivity before reporting.

item.description:
The OWASP Secure Headers Project describes HTTP response headers that your application can use to increase the security of your application. Once set, these HTTP response headers can restrict modern browsers from running into easily preventable vulnerabilities.

item.title: Slack token pattern
item.description:
Flags secret-like tokens and exposed configuration values in observed HTML/JS/JSON responses. These are recon leads; validate sensitivity before reporting.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Social Security Number
item.description: Sensitive data is anything that should not be accessible to admin access, known as sensitive data. Sensitive data may include personally identifiable information (PII), such as Social Security numbers, financial information, or login credentials. Sensitive Data Exposure occurs when an organization unknowingly exposes sensitive data or when a security incident leads to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, or admin disclosure of, or access to sensitive data.

item.title: SPA hash DOM XSS
item.description:
Tests hash-based SPA parameters (http://host/#/route?param=…) for DOM XSS by mutating the hash in a dedicated attack tab and inspecting the DOM.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Spring Boot actuator endpoint observed
item.description:
Flags high-value endpoint patterns observed in traffic (admin panels, debug endpoints, consoles, and backup/config file paths).

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: SQL Injection - Double Quote (after)
item.description: A SQL injection attack consists of insertion or injection of a SQL query via the input data from the client to the application. A successful SQL injection exploit can read sensitive data from the database, modify database data (Insert/Update/Delete), execute administration operations on the database (such as shutdown the DBMS), recover the content of a given file present on the DBMS file system and in some cases issue commands to the operating system. SQL injection attacks are a type of injection attack, in which SQL commands are injected into data-plane input in order to affect the execution of predefined SQL commands.

item.title: SQL Injection - Double Quote (before)
item.description: A SQL injection attack consists of insertion or injection of a SQL query via the input data from the client to the application. A successful SQL injection exploit can read sensitive data from the database, modify database data (Insert/Update/Delete), execute administration operations on the database (such as shutdown the DBMS), recover the content of a given file present on the DBMS file system and in some cases issue commands to the operating system. SQL injection attacks are a type of injection attack, in which SQL commands are injected into data-plane input in order to affect the execution of predefined SQL commands.

item.title: SQL Injection - Single Quote (after)
item.description: A SQL injection attack consists of insertion or injection of a SQL query via the input data from the client to the application. A successful SQL injection exploit can read sensitive data from the database, modify database data (Insert/Update/Delete), execute administration operations on the database (such as shutdown the DBMS), recover the content of a given file present on the DBMS file system and in some cases issue commands to the operating system. SQL injection attacks are a type of injection attack, in which SQL commands are injected into data-plane input in order to affect the execution of predefined SQL commands.

item.title: SQL Injection - Single Quote (before)
item.description: A SQL injection attack consists of insertion or injection of a SQL query via the input data from the client to the application. A successful SQL injection exploit can read sensitive data from the database, modify database data (Insert/Update/Delete), execute administration operations on the database (such as shutdown the DBMS), recover the content of a given file present on the DBMS file system and in some cases issue commands to the operating system. SQL injection attacks are a type of injection attack, in which SQL commands are injected into data-plane input in order to affect the execution of predefined SQL commands.

item.title: SSRF / webhook URL candidate parameter
item.description:
Flags request parameters and JSON keys commonly associated with high-impact findings (open redirect, SSRF, IDOR, file/path traversal).

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Strict-Transport-Security sent over HTTP (ineffective)
item.description:
The OWASP Secure Headers Project describes HTTP response headers that your application can use to increase the security of your application. Once set, these HTTP response headers can restrict modern browsers from running into easily preventable vulnerabilities.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Stripe publishable key exposed
item.description:
Flags secret-like tokens and exposed configuration values in observed HTML/JS/JSON responses. These are recon leads; validate sensitivity before reporting.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Swagger UI detected
item.description:
Detects exposure of API documentation, specs, and interactive consoles observed in traffic.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Swagger/OpenAPI path observed
item.description:
Flags high-value endpoint patterns observed in traffic (admin panels, debug endpoints, consoles, and backup/config file paths).

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: template.innerHTML with dynamic content
item.description:
Detects cases where untrusted data from the DOM (URL, element values, storage, messages, etc.) flows into HTML/JS execution sinks (e.g., innerHTML, outerHTML, document.write, string-based setTimeout, insertAdjacentHTML) without proper sanitization or encoding \u2014 enabling DOM-based cross-site scripting.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: Webpack dev-server / hot reload artifacts
item.description:
Detects source map references and common debug artifacts in observed HTML/JS responses. These are high-value recon leads for code disclosure and hidden endpoints.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: ws:// from HTTPS context
item.description:
Looks for common WebSocket endpoints and insecure patterns such as ws:// from HTTPS pages.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: X-Powered-By header or equivalent present
item.description:
The OWASP Secure Headers Project describes HTTP response headers that your application can use to increase the security of your application. Once set, these HTTP response headers can restrict modern browsers from running into easily preventable vulnerabilities.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: X-XSS-Protection header is a legacy directive
item.description:
The OWASP Secure Headers Project describes HTTP response headers that your application can use to increase the security of your application. Once set, these HTTP response headers can restrict modern browsers from running into easily preventable vulnerabilities.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: XSS - attribute context img onerror
item.description: Reflected Cross-site Scripting (XSS) is another name for non-persistent XSS, where the attack doesn’t load with the vulnerable web application but is originated by the victim loading the offending URI. In this article we will see some ways to test a web application for this kind of vulnerability.

item.title: XSS - attribute-name event injection
item.description: Reflected Cross-site Scripting (XSS) is another name for non-persistent XSS, where the attack doesn’t load with the vulnerable web application but is originated by the victim loading the offending URI. In this article we will see some ways to test a web application for this kind of vulnerability.

item.title: XSS - double-quoted attribute event injection
item.description: Reflected Cross-site Scripting (XSS) is another name for non-persistent XSS, where the attack doesn’t load with the vulnerable web application but is originated by the victim loading the offending URI. In this article we will see some ways to test a web application for this kind of vulnerability.

item.title: XSS - Img onerror
item.description: Reflected Cross-site Scripting (XSS) is another name for non-persistent XSS, where the attack doesn’t load with the vulnerable web application but is originated by the victim loading the offending URI. In this article we will see some ways to test a web application for this kind of vulnerability.

item.title: XSS - Img onerror
item.description: Reflected Cross-site Scripting (XSS) is another name for non-persistent XSS, where the attack doesn’t load with the vulnerable web application but is originated by the victim loading the offending URI. In this article we will see some ways to test a web application for this kind of vulnerability.

item.description: Reflected Cross-site Scripting (XSS) is another name for non-persistent XSS, where the attack doesn’t load with the vulnerable web application but is originated by the victim loading the offending URI. In this article we will see some ways to test a web application for this kind of vulnerability.

In this article we will see some ways to test a web application for this kind of vulnerability.

item.description: Reflected Cross-site Scripting (XSS) is another name for non-persistent XSS, where the attack doesn’t load with the vulnerable web application but is originated by the victim loading the offending URI. In this article we will see some ways to test a web application for this kind of vulnerability.

In this article we will see some ways to test a web application for this kind of vulnerability.

In this article we will see some ways to test a web application for this kind of vulnerability.

item.title: XSS - JS template literal break-out

In this article we will see some ways to test a web application for this kind of vulnerability.

item.title: XSS - Script tag after noscript tag
item.description: Reflected Cross-site Scripting (XSS) is another name for non-persistent XSS, where the attack doesn’t load with the vulnerable web application but is originated by the victim loading the offending URI. In this article we will see some ways to test a web application for this kind of vulnerability.

item.title: XSS - single-quoted attribute event injection
item.description: Reflected Cross-site Scripting (XSS) is another name for non-persistent XSS, where the attack doesn’t load with the vulnerable web application but is originated by the victim loading the offending URI. In this article we will see some ways to test a web application for this kind of vulnerability.

item.title: XSS - SVG onload polyglot

In this article we will see some ways to test a web application for this kind of vulnerability.

In this article we will see some ways to test a web application for this kind of vulnerability.

item.title: XSS - tag-name SVG onload injection

In this article we will see some ways to test a web application for this kind of vulnerability.

item.title: XSS - Unfiltered <script> tag

In this article we will see some ways to test a web application for this kind of vulnerability.

item.title: XSS - unquoted attribute event injection
item.description: Reflected Cross-site Scripting (XSS) is another name for non-persistent XSS, where the attack doesn’t load with the vulnerable web application but is originated by the victim loading the offending URI. In this article we will see some ways to test a web application for this kind of vulnerability.
